question: did they use a crowdsourcing platform for manual annotations?
text: The supplied evidence does not name a crowdsourcing platform or service for the manual annotations. [2] [4]

citations.title: pioNER: Datasets and Baselines for Armenian Named Entity Recognition
citations.text: The dataset provides annotations for 3 popular named entity classes: people (PER), organizations (ORG), and locations (LOC), and is released in CoNLL03 format with IOB tagging scheme. Tokens and sentences were segmented according to the UD standards for the Armenian language BIBREF11 .

citations.title: pioNER: Datasets and Baselines for Armenian Named Entity Recognition
citations.text: We propose a gold-standard corpus with manual annotation of CoNLL named entity categories: person, location, and organization BIBREF8 BIBREF9 , hoping it will be used to evaluate future named entity recognition models.

citations.title: pioNER: Datasets and Baselines for Armenian Named Entity Recognition
citations.text: We propose a gold-standard corpus with manual annotation of CoNLL named entity categories: person, location, and organization BIBREF8 BIBREF9 , hoping it will be used to evaluate future named entity recognition models.

citations.title: pioNER: Datasets and Baselines for Armenian Named Entity Recognition
citations.text: We present a 163000-token named entity corpus automatically generated and annotated from Wikipedia, and another 53400-token corpus of news sentences with manual annotation of people, organization and location named entities. The corpora were used to train and evaluate several popular named entity recognition models.

trace.question: did they use a crowdsourcing platform for manual annotations?

tasks.question: Determine whether the study used a crowdsourcing platform to obtain manual annotations.

requirements.description: Methods or data-annotation evidence stating whether annotations were performed manually and identifying who performed them.
Evidence naming any crowdsourcing platform or service used for annotation (or indicating that annotators were recruited/managed without such a platform).
